dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.Broker.Docs: Not currently supported by AWS CloudFormation.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.IncludeContro.Docs: Shows detailed control information for table definition, column definition, and table and column changes in the Kafka message output. The default is `false`.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.IncludeNullAn.Docs: Include NULL and empty columns for records migrated to the endpoint. The default is `false`.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.IncludeTableA.Docs: Includes any data definition language (DDL) operations that change the table in the control data, such as `rename-table`, `drop-table`, `add-column`, `drop-column`, and `rename-column`. The default is `false`.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.IncludeTransa.Docs: Provides detailed transaction information from the source database. This information includes a commit timestamp, a log position, and values for `transaction_id`, previous `transaction_id`, and `transaction_record_id` (the record offset within a transaction). The default is `false`.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.NoHexPrefix.Docs: Set this optional parameter to `true` to avoid adding a '0x' prefix to raw data in hexadecimal format. For example, by default, AWS DMS adds a '0x' prefix to the LOB column type in hexadecimal format moving from an Oracle source to a Kafka target. Use the `NoHexPrefix` endpoint setting to enable migration of RAW data type columns without adding the '0x' prefix.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.PartitionIncl.Docs: Prefixes schema and table names to partition values, when the partition type is `primary-key-type`. Doing this increases data distribution among Kafka partitions. For example, suppose that a SysBench schema has thousands of tables and each table has only limited range for a primary key. In this case, the same primary key is sent from thousands of tables to the same partition, which causes throttling. The default is `false`.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SaslPassword.Docs: The secure password you created when you first set up your MSK cluster to validate a client identity and make an encrypted connection between server and client using SASL-SSL authentication.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SaslUserName.Docs: The secure user name you created when you first set up your MSK cluster to validate a client identity and make an encrypted connection between server and client using SASL-SSL authentication.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SecurityProto.Docs: Set secure connection to a Kafka target endpoint using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Options include `ssl-encryption`, `ssl-authentication`, and `sasl-ssl`. `sasl-ssl` requires `SaslUsername` and `SaslPassword`.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SslCaCertific.Docs: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the private certificate authority (CA) cert that AWS DMS uses to securely connect to your Kafka target endpoint.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SslClientCert.Docs: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the client certificate used to securely connect to a Kafka target endpoint.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SslClientKeyA.Docs: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the client private key used to securely connect to a Kafka target endpoint.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.SslClientKeyP.Docs: The password for the client private key used to securely connect to a Kafka target endpoint.

dms.endpoint.kafkasettings.Topic.Docs: Not currently supported by AWS CloudFormation.
